phenomenon: Why repeated execution, inspection, and tightening can make a skill output converge instead of drifting.
causal_chain: Run the skill, observe where the output drifts, tighten the rule or renderer, then re-run with fewer degrees of freedom.
key_prerequisites: A visible output artifact that can be compared across runs.
compressed_explanation: A feedback loop stabilizes when each iteration removes a specific source of drift.
validation_question: What exactly is being constrained on each pass: data quality, rule ambiguity, or render structure?

direct_routes: When the output target itself is unclear, return to [[Single Output Target]].
If the drift is mainly scope sprawl rather than causal instability, route to [[Prompt Narrowing]].
secondary_routes: If [[Prompt Narrowing]] stabilizes the scope but the cause of drift remains unclear, continue to [[Deterministic Card Rendering]].
gap_signals: If the graph contains stable outputs but no card explains why tightening works, add a new Mechanism card for that missing causal explanation.
stop_rules: Stop following this mechanism after two hops if the target itself keeps changing; reframe the task instead.

next_goal: Add boundary cases and identify where the loop fails to stabilize.
growing_checklist: Describe at least one failure boundary.
stable_checklist: Document supporting evidence from repeated runs.
expert_ready_checklist: Add competing-mechanism arbitration and stronger diagnosis routes.
current_upgrade_tasks: Capture one anomaly where tighter constraints reduced quality instead of improving it.

main_reasons: The mechanism already works as a reliable local explanatory node.
It has early dispatch behavior but not enough competing-mechanism arbitration yet.
missing_evidence: It lacks a stronger switch rule between rendering drift and scope drift.
Its stop rules are still narrow.
next_rules: If deterministic rendering is stable but semantic drift remains, switch to a different explanatory mechanism.
When explanation is complete and the next question becomes action, route into a method card.

upgrade_history: 2026-03-27: migrated routing to the four-part dispatch structure
